title: Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup

seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of authentic Indian Mulligatawny Lentil Soup garnished with fresh cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a hearty bowl of our delicious Mulligatawny Lentil Soup, a flavorful Indian classic perfect for any day!

description: Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup is a hearty and aromatic Indian-inspired dish, famously blending lentils with a medley of vegetables and a vibrant spice profile. This comforting soup offers a delightful balance of savory, tangy, and subtly sweet flavors, making it a perfect meal for any day. Its rich texture and fragrant spices promise a truly satisfying culinary experience.

ingredients.notes: diced (Granny Smith or similar tart apple)

instructions.title: Prepare Lentils and Aromatics
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat oil or ghee over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and mustard seeds, letting them splutter for about 30 seconds. Add the bay leaf and cinnamon stick.

instructions.title: Saute Vegetables and Spices
instructions.text: Add the chopped onion to the pot and saute until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger, cooking for another minute until fragrant. Add the diced carrot and apple, cooking for 3-4 minutes until slightly softened.

instructions.title: Build the Flavor Base
instructions.text: Stir in the curry powder, turmeric powder, and tomato paste. Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to toast the spices and deepen their flavor. This step is crucial for the soup's robust taste.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Add the rinsed lentils and vegetable broth to the pot. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are tender and have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Finish with Coconut Milk and Seasoning
instructions.text: Once the lentils are cooked, stir in the full-fat coconut milk. Season the soup with salt and black pepper to taste. If you prefer a smoother consistency, you can use an immersion blender to partially blend the soup, leaving some texture.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Remove the bay leaf and cinnamon stick. Stir in the fresh lemon juice just before serving. Ladle the hot Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup into bowls and garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve immediately.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, consider adding a pinch of asafoetida (hing) with the initial tempering of the seeds.
Adjust the spice level by increasing or decreasing the curry powder, or by adding a finely chopped green chili with the ginger and garlic.
This soup tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.
article:
Few dishes tell a story quite like Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup. A delicious fusion of Indian spices and British culinary sensibilities, this vibrant and aromatic soup has captivated palates for centuries. More than just a meal, it’s a journey through history, offering warmth, comfort, and an explosion of flavor in every spoonful. If you're looking to explore the rich tapestry of Indian soups and stews, Mulligatawny Lentil Soup is an absolute must-try. Its unique blend of hearty lentils, fragrant spices, and often a touch of tang makes it a truly unforgettable culinary experience.

## What is Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup?
The name "Mulligatawny" itself is a fascinating clue to its origins, derived from the Tamil words ‘milagu’ (pepper) and ‘tanni’ (water), essentially meaning "pepper water." This classic Anglo-Indian soup originated during the British Raj, as officials sought a local equivalent to their customary meal-starting broths. While recipes vary, the essence of Mulligatawny remains consistent: a richly flavored, spicy, and often tangy soup that perfectly embodies the culinary bridge between two cultures.

Traditionally, Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup uses a base of lentils and vegetables, simmered with aromatic Indian spices like turmeric, cumin, coriander, and chili. Some versions incorporate chicken or lamb, while others remain strictly vegetarian or vegan, focusing on the earthy goodness of legumes and fresh produce. Its complex flavor profile – a delicate balance of heat, warmth, and a bright tang from ingredients like tamarind or lemon – makes it truly unique. This hearty and wholesome Mulligatawny Lentil Soup is not just nourishing; it's a flavorful adventure.

## Ingredients You Need
Creating a delicious Mulligatawny Spiced Lentil Soup starts with selecting the right ingredients. This list provides the core components for a robust, lentil-based version.

*   **Lentils:** 1 cup red lentils (masoor dal), rinsed.
*   **Aromatics:** 1 large onion (chopped), 4-5 cloves garlic (minced), 1-inch ginger (grated).
*   **Tomatoes:** 1 large, chopped, or 1/2 cup canned crushed tomatoes.
*   **Liquid:** 6-8 cups vegetable broth (or water).
*   **Optional Creaminess:** 1/2 cup full-fat coconut milk.
*   **Tang:** 2 tablespoons lemon juice, or to taste.
*   **Garnish:** 1/4 cup fresh cilantro
